hooks._comment: ADR-028 D1 guard-dispatcher pilot (mt#2650): the SOLE settings.json entry for every guard migrated onto .minsky/hooks/dispatch-pretooluse.ts's in-process registry (currently: check-guessed-session-path). Takes the migrated guard's ORIGINAL slot in this block to preserve first-deny-wins execution order relative to its siblings — do not add new individual hook commands here; register them in .minsky/hooks/registry.ts's GUARD_REGISTRY instead.

PreToolUse._comment: mt#2823 approved-Ask permission bridge. Deliberately STANDALONE (not GUARD_REGISTRY/dispatcher): it emits permissionDecision ALLOW, which the dispatcher's deny-aggregation contract does not carry — mirrors parallel-work-guard's standalone precedent. Harness deny-precedence means any sibling hook's deny still outranks this hook's allow.

mt#2029 R1 consolidation: single PreToolUse block for policy-coverage-detector covering both Claude Code native write tools and MCP session write tools. Consolidating eliminates the double-invocation hazard the prior two-block layout had (if a future matcher refactor accidentally overlapped, both blocks would fire and skew calibration). require-session-for-main-workspace-edits stays in its own native-only block above (session tools are by definition session-scoped, so its guard doesn't apply there).

hooks.statusMessage: Checking the task spec was read before advancing/binding...

PreToolUse._comment: mt#2865 dispatch-intent write gate. Deliberately STANDALONE, not GUARD_REGISTRY/dispatcher: dispatch-pretooluse.ts is registered only under the 'Bash|mcp__minsky__session_exec' matcher block above — the session_commit/session_edit_file/session_write_file/session_search_replace/session_pr_create/session_pr_edit tool family this guard gates is NOT among the tool names that already spawn the dispatcher process, so migrating would require widening THAT block's matcher (extra dispatcher spawns for every guard already registered there, for zero benefit — none of them match this tool family). A standalone registration is the cheaper correct choice here, mirroring block-subagent-merge-without-grant.ts's precedent for the sibling D5 merge guard. session_pr_merge is deliberately excluded — already D5-covered by block-subagent-merge-without-grant.ts above. Timeout=30 (PR #2033 R1 NON-BLOCKING #4): matched to parallel-work-guard.ts's timeout (also 30, below) rather than invented — both are standalone PreToolUse guards reacting to session/dispatch-lifecycle tool calls, a closer shape match than ask-permission-bridge.ts's 60 (that guard does live Ask server verification, a heavier network-bound workload this guard never does — it only reads a local JSON file, plus this PR's new exclusive-create lock retry loop, which itself caps at ~1s well inside 30).

hooks._comment: ADR-028 guard-dispatcher Stop entrypoint (mt#2357): the SOLE settings.json entry for every Stop-event guard registered in .minsky/hooks/registry.ts's GUARD_REGISTRY (currently one: turn-end-retro-scan, the framework's first Stop-event guard — advisory turn-end retrospective-trigger scan, dedup-bounded to one continuation beat per phrase). Do not add new individual Stop hook commands here; register them in GUARD_REGISTRY instead. Timeout=15 (host-cap budget model, hook-files.mdc): the family's worst-case SUM — one guard at timeoutMs 10s — plus dispatcher overhead margin. typecheck-on-stop.ts above stays deliberately standalone (predates the framework; emits its own block decisions on its own 60s budget).

hooks._comment: ADR-028 D1 guard-dispatcher Phase 2a+2b+mt#2812+mt#2824+mt#2870 (mt#2652, mt#2687, mt#2812, mt#2824, mt#2870): the SOLE settings.json entry for EVERY UserPromptSubmit guard, all migrated (or, for mt#2812/mt#2824/mt#2870, authored directly) onto .minsky/hooks/dispatch-userpromptsubmit.ts's in-process registry (Phase 2a: substrate-bypass-detector, retrospective-trigger-scanner, pre-narration-detector, causal-premise-detector, code-mechanism-assertion-detector, ask-routing-deferral-detector; Phase 2b: auto-session-title, inject-current-time, inject-git-state, inject-prod-state, inject-dispatch-watchdog, memory-search, skill-staleness-detector, mcp-daemon-staleness-detector; mt#2812: guard-health-escalation-detector; mt#2824: silent-stretch-detector; mt#2870: wall-of-text-detector; calibration-review-cadence-detector kept last per registry.ts). Do not add new individual hook commands here; register them in .minsky/hooks/registry.ts's GUARD_REGISTRY instead. Timeout=145 (host-cap budget model, hook-files.mdc "Dispatcher host-cap budget model"): this ONE process now runs all eighteen UserPromptSubmit guards SEQUENTIALLY, so the host cap must cover the family's worst-case SUM, not one guard's budget. Verified 2026-07-17 (mt#2870) by summing every UserPromptSubmit registry.ts timeoutMs: Phase 2a's six guards (substrate-bypass-detector 15s + retrospective-trigger-scanner 10s + pre-narration-detector 10s + causal-premise-detector 10s + code-mechanism-assertion-detector 10s + ask-routing-deferral-detector 10s = 65s) + Phase 2b's 8 guards (auto-session-title 5s + inject-current-time 5s + inject-git-state 5s + inject-prod-state 5s + inject-dispatch-watchdog 5s + memory-search 10s + skill-staleness-detector 5s + mcp-daemon-staleness-detector 5s = 45s) + calibration-review-cadence-detector 10s + guard-health-escalation-detector 5s + silent-stretch-detector 10s + wall-of-text-detector 10s = 65+45+10+5+10+10=145s.
